title: The Holy Spirit
description: This stained-glass window depicts the Holy Spirit as a descending dove above a rising flame. The dove recalls Christ’s baptism: “He saw the Spirit of God descending like a dove” (Matthew 3:16, ESV), revealing Jesus as the Son of God. The flame reflects Pentecost, when the Spirit came in fire (Acts 2:3, ESV), bringing life and faith to the Church. Surrounded by radiant light, the image proclaims the Spirit’s ongoing work: “He will bear witness about me” (John 15:26, ESV). Through Word and Sacrament, the Holy Spirit continues to testify to Christ, drawing all to Him.

title: The Ascension
description: This stained-glass window proclaims the Ascension of our Lord Jesus Christ. The upward movement draws the eye from the earth below to the heavens above, where Christ is exalted. The cross at the center reminds us that the One who ascends is the same who was crucified and risen for our salvation. Scripture declares, “He was lifted up, and a cloud took him out of their sight” (Acts 1:9, ESV). Now He is seated at the right hand of God—a position not of place, but of divine authority and rule. From there, Christ reigns for His Church, governing all things for the sake of His people.

title: The Empty Tomb
description: This stained-glass window proclaims the empty tomb of our Lord Jesus Christ. The stone is rolled away, the grave stands open, and the banner of victory rises, declaring His resurrection. “He is not here, for he has risen, as he said” (Matthew 28:6, ESV). The dawn breaking behind the tomb reveals the new creation begun in Christ’s triumph over sin and death. The victory banner testifies that Christ has conquered sin, death, and the devil.
